own_review_points: McpTracingInstrumenter reads mcpConfig.servers().get(McpServer.DEFAULT).tracingEnabled() once at construction -> no per-server granularity and no runtime toggling (@Singleton)
BatchAssert.doSend returns null with '// todo handle otel in batches' -> client batch tracing unimplemented
Validation-failure span hardcodes 'Schema validation failed' instead of the actual validator message
injectMcpOtelContext impl always injects Context.current() regardless of whether a span was started/tracing enabled
OTel scope activated on the event loop may not propagate to worker-thread @Tool execution (nested user spans become independent)
FeatureManagerBase resolves McpTracing eagerly via Arc.container().select in constructor (verified safe — app-scoped deps only)
notes: Diff is truncated (~113k chars past the marker) but all cited files and most code are within the visible window and verified: per-server/construction-time enabled flag on McpTracingInstrumenter (+1 and +1 for the distinct runtime-toggle concern), batch doSend todo (+1), hardcoded 'Schema validation failed' (+1), unconditional injectMcpOtelContext (+1). Worker-thread scope-propagation concern is a sound, substantive OTel/Vert.x design point on a changed file (FeatureManagerBase) (+1), though the exact executeBlocking site sits outside the shown hunks. The Arc.container().select note is self-described as 'safe/verified' -> trivial. No fabrications and no false 'missing' claims (it correctly respects the truncation). Human reference is only a maintainer discussion comment, so no concrete misses. Excellent depth on a hard, large PR.
